world.title: Woman arrested for allegedly torching ex-boyfriend’s home after breakup dispute
world.description: A 40-year-old woman was arrested in Limpopo after allegedly setting her boyfriend’s house on fire shortly after their relationship ended. The blaze destroyed property worth R45,000, and the suspect was later tracked down at a taxi rank while allegedly trying to flee.

world.title: US-sanctioned tankers pass Strait of Hormuz despite US blockade, data shows
world.description: The Rich Starry would be the first vessel to pass through the strait and exit the Gulf since the blockade began, data from LSEG, MarineTraffic and Kpler showed.

world.title: Gavin Newsom trolls Trump for his defence of Christ photo - 'Does this look like a Red Cross worker to you?'
world.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom has trolled Donald Trump after the president said he thought his Jesus Christ photo was of him as a doctor, healing people. Newsom's press office shared a photo of Christ in a white robe and red sash, and asked if he looks like a Red Cross worker.

world.title: Eurozone services production rises at start of 2026
world.description: Eurostat has reported that services production increased in both the euro area and the European Union in January 2026, reflecting a continued recovery in the sector. The first estimates showed that seasonally adjusted services output rose by 1.2 per cent in the euro area and by 0.6 per cent in…

world.title: Somaliland holds maritime security meeting amid red sea tensions
world.description: Hargeisa (HOL) — Somaliland’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs convened a quarterly meeting of the Maritime Security Coordination Mechanism on Saturday, bringing together key government institutions to assess emerging threats and strengthen coordination along its coastline.

world.title: Google expands Gemini practice tests to NEET UG, adds mock exam support
world.description: Google has introduced NEET UG practice tests in Gemini, expanding its exam prep tools with mock exams based on vetted content to help students prepare in a structured and accessible format.

business.title: Ogilvy Group HK names Duffy Lau as CEO, consolidates leadership across Ogilvy and Grey
business.description: The Ogilvy Group Hong Kong has promoted Duffy Lau to Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately, as it strengthens leadership across its two agency brands, Ogilvy and Grey.

business.title: Oil prices ease on hopes of new US
business.description: Oil prices dipped in early Asian trade on Tuesday as hopes of more peace talks between the US and Iran eased concerns about further disruptions to energy supplies.

business.description: The overall bias for USD/CHF has turned decidedly bearish in the short to medium term following a significant rejection at the 0.80000 psychological handle.

business.title: Mideast war revs up electric car demand in Asia
business.description: Electric vehicle sales have jumped in Southeast Asia as cost-conscious buyers have poured into dealerships looking to dodge the fuel price spikes driven by the Middle East war.

business.title: Nissan to trim global car lineup, boost use of AI driving tech
business.description: Nissan Motor plans to streamline its global automobile lineup by exiting low-performing ones and deploying AI technology over 90% of its array in the long term.

business.title: Amazon worker dies on warehouse floor; colleagues continue working around his body - ‘Let’s get back to work’
business.description: An Amazon employee at the Troutdale warehouse died while working, reportedly unnoticed for over an hour. The incident has raised safety concerns among employees, with claims of high temperatures and ongoing operations despite the emergency. The exact cause of death remains unknown.

science.title: This strange “pearling” motion inside cells could change how we understand disease
science.description: Mitochondria don’t just generate energy—they also carefully organize their own DNA in a surprisingly elegant way. Scientists have discovered that a long-overlooked phenomenon called “mitochondrial pearling,” where mitochondria briefly form bead-like shapes, helps evenly space clusters of mitochondrial DNA.
